acceptanceGates: New API is used as a capability and behavior reference, not as the Elygate runtime kernel.
Every public New API route surface must either be implemented compatibly or tracked as a strict-gate blocker.
API compatibility must not require New API database, Redis, queue, cache, or runtime internals.
No Redis server, Redis client, or Redis cache package may become a default Elygate runtime dependency.
Each absorbed capability must be implemented with PostgreSQL-native storage or an explicitly feature-flagged, reversible adapter.
Hot-path replacement of auth, rate limit, billing, cache, queue, or LISTEN/NOTIFY requires benchmark, parity check, smoke test, rollback note, and bun run check evidence.
Protocol changes require route or handler tests that verify request shape, response shape, streaming behavior when applicable, and billing/logging side effects.

domains.title: OpenAI-compatible protocol surface
nextActions: Keep strict route parity and protocol golden tests in root check.
Track WebRTC Realtime bridge separately from the current WebSocket proxy if product demand appears.
domains.postgresNativePlan: Protocol state, files, batches, logs, and billing stay in PostgreSQL-backed tables or pg-boss jobs.
domains.redisFreeGate: No Redis cache or queue may be added for protocol compatibility.

domains.title: Provider and media/task breadth
nextActions: Keep task-style media providers backed by PostgreSQL job state and pg-boss-compatible dispatch.
Rank any future provider additions by active customer need before adding adapters.
domains.postgresNativePlan: Async provider tasks use PostgreSQL job state and pg-boss, not Redis streams.
domains.redisFreeGate: Task polling, retry, and callback state must not require Redis.

domains.title: Routing, channel health, and admin operations
nextActions: Keep channel copy, upstream model sync, key strategy, error mapping, retry group, and rate-limit regressions in root check.
Re-run admin channel DB smoke before release candidates.
domains.postgresNativePlan: Routing state, channel status, key status, and test history remain in PostgreSQL tables with in-process cache invalidation.
domains.redisFreeGate: Redis may not be introduced for channel affinity, status cache, or retry counters.

domains.title: Identity, billing, payment, and quota product features
nextActions: Keep public commerce features disabled unless explicit product and compliance configuration enables them.
Keep enterprise quota, subscription lifecycle, and webhook idempotency fixtures in the compat suite.
domains.postgresNativePlan: Quota, invoices, subscriptions, redemption codes, and webhook state use transactional PostgreSQL tables.
domains.redisFreeGate: Payment idempotency and webhook replay protection must be PostgreSQL-backed, with no Redis requirement.

domains.title: Security, verification, and compliance controls
nextActions: Keep Panel versus Enterprise ownership boundaries explicit when adding new security controls.
Keep email, captcha, OIDC, two-factor, passkey, audit, and log-retention checks backed by mocked secrets or temporary PostgreSQL state.
domains.postgresNativePlan: Security events, audit trails, and verification state remain in PostgreSQL with secret redaction in logs.
domains.redisFreeGate: Verification code TTL must be PostgreSQL-backed or local dev-only in-memory, never Redis-only.

domains.title: Redis-free platform boundary
newApiSignals: Redis connection string is recommended for cache in New API
Redis shared deployments require a crypto secret
nextActions: Keep Redis absence as a release gate.
Use @postgresx/noredis only behind subsystem-specific flags and benchmarks.
domains.postgresNativePlan: PostgreSQL remains the only required external state service.
domains.redisFreeGate: Root package manifests and compose files must not add Redis runtime dependencies or services.
